title: "They Were All Filled With The Holy Ghost"  Acts 4:31

notes.introduction: An exposition of Acts 4:31 on the filling of the Holy Ghost, tracing the two occurrences of the phrase in Acts 4 (vv. 8 and 31) against the backdrop of the healing in Acts 3, the disciples' arrest, and their prayer for boldness. The distinction between the indwelling and the continuous filling of the Spirit is developed through Ephesians 5:18, John 14-16, and Galatians 5.
themes: the holy ghost in the church age

sections.heading: Two Occurrences of 'Filled with the Holy Ghost' in Acts 4
Point 1: God's Plan for Every Believer to Be Filled
Point 2: A Privilege Unique to New Testament Believers
The Holy Ghost's Work in the Lost and in Believers
Point 3: God Is Pleased When Believers Are Filled
Stephen as Example of Being Full of the Holy Ghost
